title: T10 Fleet: Roamy as Swagger worker
prompt: Prove the cross-machine delegation seam (decisions.md Q8): one real ticket claimed on the Mini (rename CAS queue/ -> running/ with claimed_by stamped), executed on Roamy over Tailscale SSH (target is 'roamy' = 100.89.32.35 — NOT the seed's 100.82.235.39, which is the Mini itself; never .local), and the artifact verified back on the Mini. The mechanic was live-proven at authoring 2026-07-06: ssh davidcruwys@roamy works batch-mode, claude 2.1.201 sits at ~/.local/bin/claude (login-shell PATH only — use absolute paths for claude and /opt/homebrew/bin/tmux), it is subscription-authed with zero ANTHROPIC env vars, and a detached-tmux interactive claude boots in ~5s ('bypass permissions on' signature) and answers a send-keys turn in ~5s. You will author a small payload ticket (wg-t10-01p-roamy-echo.json), claim it, scp it to an untracked engine/store/remote/ workspace on Roamy, boot tmux session df-roamy-1 running interactive claude (NEVER -p/headless/SDK — metered billing; abort A1 if any ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN exists on Roamy or claude there is not subscription-authed), dispatch a quote-free pointer prompt, poll for the result artifact, scp it back to engine/store/results/, assert its hostname field says 'Roamy', move the ticket to done/, append an audit.jsonl line naming the remote worker and its Roamy-side transcript, tear down the tmux session and remote workspace leaving Roamy's tree clean, and commit+push the evidence. Productization (pool/CAP/routing) is T10-06 — out of scope. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T10-01-roamy-swagger-worker.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write engine/store/needs-decision/ per the war game, leaving the ticket in running/.
verify: On the Mini: engine/store/results/wg-t10-01p-roamy-echo.json parses as JSON with status 'done', hostname exactly 'Roamy', and a >=7-char head_sha; engine/store/done/wg-t10-01p-roamy-echo.json exists with claimed_by 'mac-mini-m4-wg-t10-01-dispatch' and the same filename is absent from queue/ and running/; the last lines of engine/store/audit.jsonl contain 'wg-t10-01p-roamy-echo'; a commit whose message references wg-t10-01 is on main. On Roamy (over ssh davidcruwys@roamy, batch mode): no tmux session matching df-roamy remains, ~/dev/ad/apps/dark-factory/engine/store/remote/ does not exist, and git status --short shows nothing under engine/store/. Negative: engine/orchestrator.py, engine/warm_pool.py and engine/halt.py unmodified on the Mini; the pre-existing deferred project-digest ticket still sits in queue/; no HALT flag present; Roamy's repo still on branch main with no new local commits.
